```{r}
# emmeans_plot_2025 <- plot_emmeans_species_treatment(fit_zipfull_treatment_25, "2025")
# emmeans_plot_2025

```


#difference in observed insect visits (relative amount of visits in each year)

```{r}
#2024 relative amounts of observed insect visits
relative.insects.24 <- vrate.24.complete %>%   count(insect_class) %>%
  mutate(relative_proportion = n / sum(n))

#2025
relative.insects.25 <- vrate.25.complete %>%   count(insect_class) %>%
  mutate(relative_proportion = n / sum(n))

#NOTE: proportion of visits per insect_class is not different between years. 

```

#relative insect visits per plant species
```{r}

#Relative proportion of all insect visits to plant species (NOT STADARDIZED by flower number)
#proportion of visits by an insect per year out of that year's total
relative.global.by.year <- all.years.vrate %>% 
  group_by(year, plant, insect_class) %>% 
  summarise(total_visits = sum(visits), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  group_by(year) %>% 
  mutate(relative_proportion = round(total_visits / sum(total_visits), 3)) %>% 
  ungroup()

ggplot(relative.global.by.year,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_proportion,
           fill = insect_class)) +
  geom_col() +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = palette_gradient) +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative proportion of all visits",
    fill = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

#Standardize relative proportion of visits to plants by flower number
#Standardize within plant first, then global to remove plant abundance effects but still compare insects
relative.by.plant.flower <- all.years.vrate %>% 
  group_by(year, plant, insect_class) %>% 
  summarise(
    total_visits = sum(visits),
    flowers = mean(flower_number),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(visits_per_flower = total_visits / flowers) %>% 
  group_by(year, plant) %>% 
  mutate(relative_within_plant = visits_per_flower / sum(visits_per_flower)) %>% 
  ungroup()

#DON'T USE! only for visualization purposes - use relative.global.plant.flower.by.year below
ggplot(relative.by.plant.flower,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_within_plant,
           fill = insect_class)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative visits per flower",
    fill = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )


#USE THIS: 
#For a single year, what fraction of all flower-standardized visits went to each plant × insect combination, relative to the total for that year?
#Insect visits to plants standardized by flower number
#global proportion of insect visits to each plant species within a single year that is standardized by flower number
##Sum the visits_per_flower across all plants for each insect class within a year.
##Divide by the total visits_per_flower for that year to get the proportion.

relative.global.plant.flower.by.year <- relative.by.plant.flower %>%
                      group_by(year, plant, insect_class) %>% #all standardized visits per plant × insect are summed
                      summarise(total_visits_per_flower = sum(visits_per_flower),
                                  .groups = "drop") %>%
                      group_by(year) %>% #the denominator is all visits per flower in that year, across all plants and insects
                      mutate(relative_proportion = round(total_visits_per_flower / sum(total_visits_per_flower), 3)) %>%
                      ungroup()


ggplot(relative.global.plant.flower.by.year,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_proportion,
           fill = insect_class)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative visits per flower",
    fill = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),        # remove vertical lines
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray80", linewidth = 0.3), # horizontal lines
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggplot(relative.global.plant.flower.by.year,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_proportion,
           color = insect_class,
           group = insect_class)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative visits per flower",
    color = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw()


```


#network
##2024
```{r}

net.2024 <- vrate.24.complete %>% 
            group_by(plant, insect_class) %>% 
            summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% 
            drop_na(plant) %>% 
            pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
            arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
            column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
            relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars()))  #orders columns alphabetically

#bipartite network plot:                          
pp.network.24 <- plotweb(sqrt(net.2024), empty=F, text.rot=90, method="normal")

#network statistics
net.24.stats <- networklevel(net.2024) 



```

##2025
```{r}
net.2025 <- vrate.25.complete %>% 
            group_by(plant, insect_class) %>% 
            summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% 
            drop_na(plant) %>% 
            pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
            arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
            column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
            relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars()))  #orders columns alphabetically

#bipartite network plot:                          
pp.network.25 <- plotweb(
  sqrt(net.2025),
  empty = FALSE,
  text.rot = 90,
  method = "normal",
)

#network statistics
net.25.stats <- networklevel(net.2025) 
```

#Network rareification
downsample networks to 1000 rows for each treatment in each year

-determine smallest network size
-rarefy within year × treatment networks
-generate 500 replicate networks
-convert to matrices
-calculate network metrics
-analyze distributions of metrics across replicates

```{r}

#Creates networks for each treatment in each year 
#NOTE: creating week level networks doesn't generate enough interaction data to run networklevel() on.

#Define species list
plants <- sort(unique(all.years.vrate$plant))
pollinators <- sort(unique(all.years.vrate$insect_class))

#Rarefaction depth:
rarefaction.depth <- all.years.vrate %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2024, 2025),
         treatment %in% c("control","rainout")) %>%
  count(year, treatment)

min.rows.for.rarefaction <- min(rarefaction.depth$n)
min.rows.for.rarefaction #1349 rows

#target rows need to be lower than min.rows.for.rarefaction
target.rows <- 1000


#Rarefaction funtion (within each network)
#Rarefies within each year × treatment network before aggregation

rarefy.network.function <- function(df, sample_size, rep_id) {

  df %>%
    filter(year %in% c(2024, 2025),
           treatment %in% c("control","rainout")) %>%
    
    group_by(year, treatment) %>%
    
    group_modify(~{

      sampled <- .x %>%
        slice_sample(n = sample_size, replace = FALSE)

      aggregated <- sampled %>%
        group_by(plant, insect_class) %>%
        summarise(visits = sum(visits), .groups = "drop")

      full_grid <- expand_grid(
        plant = plants,
        insect_class = pollinators
      )

      full_grid %>%
        left_join(aggregated,
                  by = c("plant","insect_class")) %>%
        mutate(
          visits = replace_na(visits,0),
          replicate = rep_id
        )
    }) %>%
    
    ungroup()
}

#generate rarefied networks
# with n number of replicate networks
#output structure: year, treatment, plant, insect_class, visits, replicate number

n_reps <- 500

rarefied.networks <- map_dfr(
  1:n_reps,
  ~rarefy.network.function(
      df = all.years.vrate,
      sample_size = target.rows,
      rep_id = .x)
)

#Convert to plant × pollinator network matrices (stats)
pp.matrix.function <- function(df){

  df %>%
    select(plant, insect_class, visits) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = insect_class,
                values_from = visits,
                values_fill = 0) %>%
    column_to_rownames("plant") %>%
    as.matrix()
}


#Build matrix list for each network replicate
#Each element = one network matrix
#500 replicates × 4 networks = 2000 matrices

matrices.list <- rarefied.networks %>%
  group_by(year, treatment, replicate) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map(pp.matrix.function)

#Calculate network metrics (stats) using bipartite package

#library(bipartite)

network.metrics.all <- matrices.list %>%
  map_df(~{

    mat <- .x

    mat <- mat[rowSums(mat) > 0, colSums(mat) > 0]

    if(nrow(mat) < 2 | ncol(mat) < 2 | sum(mat) < 3){
      return(tibble(
        connectance = NA,
        NODF = NA,
        H2 = NA,
        links_per_species = NA
      ))
    }

    metrics <- networklevel(
      mat,
      index = c("connectance","NODF","H2","links per species")
    )

    as_tibble(as.list(metrics))
  })

#Attach metadata back to metrics
network.ids <- rarefied.networks %>%
  distinct(year, treatment, replicate) %>%
  arrange(year, treatment, replicate)

network.metrics.all.final <- bind_cols(network.ids, network.metrics.all) %>% 
                              rename(links_per_species = `links per species`)

#Mean network metrics per treatment:
mean.network.matrics.per.treatment <- network.metrics.all.final %>%
  group_by(year, treatment) %>%
  summarise(across(connectance:links_per_species,
            #alternative if you add more network metrics replace above line with:
            #across(where(is.numeric) & !replicate, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
           \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

```

##LMM network metrics

statistical comparison of network metrics across treatments and years  
handle the replicate rarefactions to avoid pseudoreplication)

500 rarefied networks per year × treatment, so each metric has a distribution of values

treats replicate rarefactions as repeated measurements of the same underlying network, not independent ecological replicates.--> use mixed-effects model with fix & random effects to control for repeated measures

```{r}

connectance.model <- lmer(
  connectance ~ year * treatment + (1 | replicate), #replicate does not contribute to the model boundary is singlular warning, try t-test for nonparametric analysis
  data = network.metrics.all.final)

#p-value is very small since there are many replicates, doesn't mean much.
Anova(connectance.model, type = "III")

nodf.mod <- lmer(
  NODF ~ year * treatment + (1 | replicate),
  data = network.metrics.all.final)
Anova(nodf.mod, type = "III")

h2.mod <- lmer(
  H2 ~ year * treatment + (1 | replicate),
  data = network.metrics.all.final)
Anova(h2.mod, type = "III")

links.mod <- lmer(
  links_per_species ~ year * treatment + (1 | replicate),
  data = network.metrics.all.final)
Anova(links.mod, type = "III")

#plot 
#change out connectance with different metrics 
#shows the uncertainty introduced by rarefaction
ggplot(network.metrics.all.final,
       aes(treatment, connectance, fill = treatment)) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~year)

#emmeans
library(emmeans)

#treatment differences within each year
#connectance, H2 emmeans contrasts significant for treatment in both years.  
#Only links per species in 2024 (not 2025), nodf emmeans contrasts no significant
emmeans(connectance.model, pairwise ~ treatment | year)
emmeans(nodf.mod, pairwise ~ treatment | year)
emmeans(h2.mod, pairwise ~ treatment | year)
emmeans(links.mod, pairwise ~ treatment | year)

#year differences within each treatment:
#Summary: all metrics shows year difference P<0.001
emmeans(connectance.model, pairwise ~ year | treatment)
emmeans(nodf.mod, pairwise ~ year | treatment)
emmeans(h2.mod, pairwise ~ year | treatment)
emmeans(links.mod, pairwise ~ year | treatment)

```

##glmmTMB for rarefied networks
```{r}

glmm.full.downsample.net <- glmmTMB(
  visits ~ year * treatment * plant + (1|insect_class),
  ziformula = ~1,
  family = nbinom2,
  data = downsampled.network)

summary(glmm.full.downsample.net)

Anova(glmm.full.downsample.net, type = "III")

#post-hoc comparisons using emmeans

#Treatment effect within each year
emmeans(glmm.full.downsample.net, pairwise ~ treatment | year)

#Year effect within each treatment
emmeans(glmm.full.downsample.net, pairwise ~ year | treatment)

```


###2024
```{r}

#down sampling allows you to have same number of interactions in both control and drought so you can compare the two networks more easily
#take net.2024 and net.2025 and take random rows

#number to down sample to
#figure out number to downsample to:
vrate.24.complete %>% count(treatment)
vrate.25.complete %>% count(treatment)

downsample.rows <- 1000 #number of rows in down sampled networks
#down sample number based on number of interactions in 2024 control 

#make mini networks 

downsample.function.2024 <- function(manipulation, downsample.rows){
                          vrate.24.complete %>%  
                          #left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                          filter(treatment== manipulation) %>% 
                          slice_sample(n=downsample.rows) %>% 
                          group_by(plant, insect_class) %>% summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% drop_na(plant) %>% 
                          pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
                          arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
                          column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
                          relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars())) %>%  #orders columns alphabetically
                          networklevel(index=c("connectance", "NODF", "H2", "links per species")) %>% 
                          t() %>% data.frame()
                        }

stats.24.downsample <- tibble(permutation=rep(1:99, times=2), treatment=rep(c("control", "rainout"), each=99)) %>% 
              mutate(stats= map(treatment, ~downsample.function.2024(.x, downsample.rows))) %>% unnest(stats)

# Links per species plot (species = insect class and size)
dnet.color.palette <- c(control="cornflowerblue", rainout="orange")
year.color.palette <- c("2024" = "darkgreen", "2025"= "purple")


ggplot(stats.24.downsample, aes(x=links.per.species, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)

ggplot(stats.24.downsample, aes(x=connectance, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)



#t test
#don't use pvalue bc we are just choosing how much data to create with number of permutations
anova(lm(links.per.species~treatment, data=stats.24.downsample))



```

###2025
```{r}
downsample.function.2025 <- function(manipulation, downsample.rows){
                          vrate.25.complete %>%  
                          #left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                          filter(treatment== manipulation) %>% 
                          #randomly selects a subset of rows
                          slice_sample(n=downsample.rows) %>% 
                          #aggregate visits 
                          group_by(plant, insect_class) %>%
                          summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% 
                          #removes missing plant names
                          drop_na(plant) %>% 
                          #convert to an interaction matrix
                          pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
                          arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
                          #column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
                          #relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars())) %>%  #orders columns alphabetically
                          #networklevel(index=c("connectance", "NODF", "H2", "links per species")) %>% 
                          #t() %>% data.frame()
                          print(head(downsampled_data))  # Debugging line
                        }

    test_output <- downsample.function.2025("control", downsample.rows)
    print(test_output)  # Check the structure and columns of the output

downsample.25 <- tibble(permutation = rep(1:99, times = 2), 
                              treatment = rep(c("control", "rainout"), each = 99)) %>% 
  mutate(stats = map(treatment, ~downsample.function.2025(.x, downsample.rows))) %>% 
  unnest(stats, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%  # Keep empty rows if any
  select(plant, insect_class, visits, permutation, treatment)  # Select relevant columns

#####
#999 permutations for each treatment
stats.25.downsample <- tibble(permutation=rep(1:99, times=2), treatment=rep(c("control", "rainout"), each=99)) %>% 
                        mutate(stats= map(treatment, ~downsample.function.2025(.x, downsample.rows))) %>% 
                        unnest(stats) #Expands the list column so each permutation becomes a normal row with network metrics

# Links per species plot (species = insect class and size - TODO: does it? might need to double check)
ggplot(stats.25.downsample, aes(x=links.per.species, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)

ggplot(stats.25.downsample, aes(x=connectance, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)

```
